dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.6 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Usage windows are even less likely to read wrong now. v11.5 anchored each progressbar to its smallest labelling ancestor and dropped per-model weekly bars; this release adds the safety net that was missing for a degenerate DOM shape — when a single wrapper carries both the "current session" label and the "weekly" label (so the scraper can't tell which window the progressbar belongs to), the slot is now surfaced as Unavailable instead of guessed. Follow-up to issue #6.
Fixed an edge case where a compact DOM wrapper containing both 5h and 7d labels could still feed the 7d slot a wrong percentage (follow-up to issue #6).

The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
